checks.finding: REQ-004/AC-008 gate cross-model verification on 'Risk: critical or an explicit security-sensitive marking,' but no artifact defines the marking's field name, syntax, or values. Two implementers could invent different tag syntaxes for the same behavior, so a task could silently skip cross-model verification under one implementation.

No direct conflict between Goals, Non-goals, Acceptance Criteria, or Security Boundaries. The REQ-004 vs lite-track-non-goal interaction is scoped by AC-009 requiring lite-track behavior for critical tasks to be documented.
Edge Cases mandates a vacuous pass when no PR is created, but AC-011/TEST-011 only obligate fail-on-violation and pass-on-compliant fixtures; the no-PR path has no acceptance criterion.
All four Assumptions are resolved by an explicit prior decision or a deterministic implementation-time verification with a fallback (REQ-005 OIDC check anchored to pinned SHA v1.0.165).

REQ-004 allows skipping cross-model only with a human waiver, but tasks.md is not a protected human-copy surface, so nothing prevents the shipping agent from writing the waiver field itself; AC-008/AC-009 test only presence, not human authorship.

Two product/security decisions (the marking field syntax and the waiver human-only mechanism) would otherwise fall to the downstream reviewer to invent rather than verify, blocking a clean handoff on AC-008/AC-009.
